title: Lordhowea velleioides
intro: Lordhowea velleioides, also known as Lord Howe's tree, is a species of tree native to Australia and the Lord Howe Island. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Lord Howe's tree include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory problems and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Lordhowea velleioides is classified within the family Proteaceae and belongs to the genus Lordhowea.
